_comment: Every trigger type spans the full commodity universe — all five clusters. Macro events propagate through the interconnected web at different speeds and attenuations; the classifier and models handle magnitude/decay, not scope restriction. The affected_commodities field is left empty and resolved entirely from affected_clusters at load time.

triggers.description: Federal Open Market Committee rate hike, cut, or hold. Primary shock: USD complex, rates-sensitive metals, credit spreads. Secondary transmission: energy (USD-denominated), agriculture (financing costs, EM demand), livestock (feed financing), digital (risk-off flows).

mild.label: In-line with market pricing (< 10 bps surprise)

severe.label: Policy shock (> 25 bps surprise or hawkish pivot)

triggers.description: BLS monthly Consumer Price Index release. Primary shock: metals (real-rate repricing), energy (inflation expectations). Secondary transmission: agriculture (input cost inflation), livestock (feed + transport costs), digital (inflation hedge flows vs risk-off).

BLS monthly employment situation. Primary shock: metals (USD/rate expectations). Secondary transmission: energy (demand outlook), agriculture (consumer spending on food), livestock (protein demand), digital (risk sentiment).

OPEC+ ministerial meeting output. Primary shock: energy complex. Secondary transmission: metals (smelting/mining energy costs), agriculture (fertilizer = natural gas, machinery = diesel), livestock (transport + heating energy), digital (energy-intensive mining cost).

moderate.label: Cut or increase within expected range

severe.label: Surprise cut > 1 mb/d or emergency meeting

triggers.description: EIA Petroleum Status Report. Primary shock: crude and products. Secondary transmission: metals (industrial activity proxy), agriculture (diesel/fertilizer cost signal), livestock (transport), digital (minor macro sentiment).

triggers.display_name: EIA Weekly Natural Gas Storage Report
triggers.description: EIA natural gas storage weekly change. Primary shock: Henry Hub and power/heating complex. Secondary transmission: agriculture (ammonia/fertilizer), metals (smelting costs), livestock (heating), digital (minor).

USDA World Agricultural Supply and Demand Estimates. Primary shock: grains and oilseeds. Secondary transmission: livestock (feed costs), energy (ethanol/biofuel demand from corn/soy), metals (agricultural equipment demand), digital (macro sentiment via food-CPI).

mild.label: Ending stocks within ±2% of pre-report estimates

triggers.description: FOMC press conference, Jackson Hole, or unscheduled Fed communication. Primary shock: metals (gold/silver safe-haven repricing). Secondary transmission: energy (USD effect), agriculture (EM FX/financing), livestock (credit costs), digital (risk-on/off).

moderate.label: Nuanced pivot language or new forward guidance

severe.label: Explicit pivot signal or emergency communication

triggers.description: DXY z-score spike. All physically-settled USD-denominated commodities reprice inversely. Primary shock: metals and energy. Secondary transmission: agriculture (export competitiveness, EM purchasing power), livestock (export demand), digital (USD alternative flows).

Sanctions, conflict escalation, or supply-route disruption. Primary shock: energy and metals (strategic resources). Secondary transmission: agriculture (Black Sea wheat, fertilizer supply), livestock (feed grain disruption), digital (safe-haven demand, mining region risk).

mild.label: Tail risk elevated; no confirmed supply loss

moderate.label: Confirmed partial supply disruption (< 1 mb/d WTI-eq)

severe.label: Major route closure or multi-country sanctions

triggers.description: FRED USREC=1 regime override. Demand destruction propagates through the entire commodity web: energy demand collapses first, metals follow (industrial slowdown), agriculture softens (consumer retrenchment), livestock contracts (protein demand elasticity), digital correlates with broad risk-off.

ENSO MEI threshold breach. Primary shock: agriculture (crop yield disruption) and energy (heating/cooling demand). Secondary transmission: livestock (feed grain prices), metals (hydroelectric power for smelting, mining access), digital (energy-cost impact on crypto mining).

Composite battery-metals / ETS / uranium signal. Primary shock: metals (EV demand) and energy (carbon pricing). Secondary transmission: agriculture (electrification of farm equipment, biofuel competition with EVs), livestock (cold-chain electrification), digital (energy cost structure shift).

moderate.label: Two sub-signals active or one strongly elevated

triggers.description: BLS Producer Price Index monthly release. Leading indicator for input cost pressure across all sectors. Primary shock: energy and metals. Secondary transmission: agriculture (input costs), livestock (feed + processing costs), digital (hardware/infrastructure cost signal via metals component).

severe.label: > 0.5 pp print or YoY inflection reversal
